title: Collapse redundant `ga′` scope selection in `MLF.Elab.Run.Scope`
problem: `src/MLF/Elab/Run/Scope.hs:36` already documents that step 2 of `resolveCanonicalScope` is “redundant but harmless”: `bindingScopeRef` finds the nearest `GenRef` from the original binding path, then `preferGenScope` immediately recomputes the same `bindingPathToRoot` on the same `Constraint` before `canonicalizeScopeRef` runs (`src/MLF/Elab/Run/Scope.hs:92`, `src/MLF/Elab/Run/Scope.hs:204`). The only extra surface this currently buys is a dedicated regression hook in `test/ScopeSpec.hs:23`. The completed `TODO.md`/`CHANGELOG.md` work covers other helper dedups, parser scaffolding, solved-facade cleanup, and fallback retirement, but not retiring this duplicate ga-scope pass.
why_this_is_simpler: Make `bindingScopeRef` the single pre-canonical `ga′` selector, delete `preferGenScope`, and have `resolveCanonicalScope` canonicalize `bindingScopeRef`’s answer directly. If wanted, keep one tiny shared helper for the common “first `GenRef` after `drop 1`, else `TypeRef root`” selection used by `bindingScopeRef` and `bindingScopeRefCanonical`. That removes one whole extra tree walk, one branch, and one helper whose behavior is already subsumed by its caller.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: The thesis defines subterm typing/environments from the gen-node abstractions attached to the original presolution structure, not from a second recovery pass (`papers/these-finale-english.txt:13631`, `papers/these-finale-english.txt:13739`). `bindingScopeRef` already computes exactly the nearest gen ancestor in the original constraint, matching the module’s own Def. 15.3.2 alignment note (`src/MLF/Elab/Run/Scope.hs:36`). Removing `preferGenScope` does not change which scope is chosen; it only stops re-deriving the same answer.
expected_benefits: Eliminates a redundant second `bindingPathToRoot` traversal in every `resolveCanonicalScope` call.
Makes the invariant explicit that nearest-gen selection happens once, before redirect/UF canonicalization.
Shrinks the scope helper surface and removes a test that currently exists only to pin a redundant helper.
Keeps the change tightly local to ga-scope handling, with no new compatibility surface.
risks: A hidden caller could still rely on `preferGenScope` as a separately exported utility, so call sites need a quick repo-wide check.
If the cleanup also factors `bindingScopeRefCanonical`, it must preserve current cycle-detection behavior and error propagation.
Source-guard tests may need small updates if they mention the old helper name explicitly.
required_validation: `rg -n '\\bpreferGenScope\\b' src test app src-public` shows no remaining live callers except any intentionally updated guard text.
`cabal test mlf2-test --test-show-details=direct --test-options='--match "ga scope|elab-input absolute thesis-exact guard"'` passes.
